Conoclinium dasytrichum, also known as Button Snakeroot or Button Aster, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The bioactive constituents of Conoclinium dasytrichum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Conoclinium dasytrichum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects.
